collectionOverview.empty_sub: Run parsing to extract the forensic timeline from this collection.

profile.title: My Profile
profile.display_name_ph: Leave empty to use full name
profile.tz_utc_desc: Recommended — prevents errors during cross-timezone correlations
profile.density_hint: Applies to data tables: cases list, evidence, IOCs, audit log, Super Timeline. Does not affect the Dashboard (widgets and cards).

profile.title: Profile
profile.desc: Identity used on cases, chain of custody, and audit logs.
profile.display_name_desc: Shown on cases, comments, and on-call rotation. Stored in audit records.
profile.role_desc: Determines available capabilities and queues. Changes require admin approval.
profile.timezone_desc: The UI follows this setting; raw data is always stored in UTC.
profile.chat_color_desc: Used to identify you in chat and pings.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.desc: Personal alert preferences. Synced with your account.
notifications.desktop_desc: Browser notifications for critical alerts and direct mentions.
notifications.critical_desc: Critical detections and urgent deadlines on your cases.
notifications.mentions_desc: When an analyst mentions you in chat or a comment.
notifications.oncall_desc: SEV-1 alerts during your on-call rotations.

sla.title: SLA
sla.desc: Global deadline bands used to classify case urgency (Triage + Dashboard). Applies to all analysts.
sla.order_hint: Thresholds must increase: urgent < watch < upcoming.

roles.title: Roles & access
roles.desc: Capabilities by role. Restrictions are enforced server-side (requireRole); the interface is only a reflection.

capabilities.view_all_cases: View all cases, not only assigned cases

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Analyst shortcut reference. The application is designed keyboard-first.
shortcuts.close_active: Close the active panel / modal / drawer

appearance.theme_desc: Calm palette, smooth transitions. Density and accent stay consistent.

team.title: Team
team.desc: Analyst accounts: roles, activation, passwords. Changes are traced in the audit log.

api_keys.title: API keys
api_keys.desc: Programmatic access tokens linked to your account. The token is shown only once at creation; only its fingerprint is stored.
api_keys.created_notice: ✓ Token created. Copy it now; it will never be shown again:
api_keys.name: Name

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.desc: Active sessions (refresh tokens) across all your browsers and devices.
sessions.revoked_msg: ✓ {{count}} session(s) revoked. The current session remains valid until the access token expires

audit.title: Audit log
audit.desc: Immutable traceability of platform actions (logins, cases, evidence, rules…). Each entry is sealed with HMAC-SHA256.
audit.verify_hint: Recomputes HMAC fingerprints and reports any tampering.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.desc: IOC enrichment API keys. Stored server-side (system_settings); environment variables (.env) remain priority when defined. Enrichment cache: 24h.
integrations.empty_hint: Saving an emptied field deletes the stored key. Unmodified fields stay unchanged.

taxii.title: Threat Intel feeds (TAXII / OpenCTI)
taxii.desc: Pull STIX indicators from any TAXII 2.1 server. OpenCTI exposes such a feed — use the preset and paste its TAXII URL + API token.

misp.title: MISP instances
misp.desc: Connect MISP instances. Exportable attributes (to_ids) are pulled and indexed alongside TAXII indicators.
misp.verify_ssl_hint: uncheck only for an internal self-signed MISP

greynoise.label: GreyNoise
greynoise.desc: Background noise classification for IPs — suppresses mass-scanner alerts.

urlhaus.label: URLhaus (abuse.ch)
urlhaus.desc: Known malware distribution URLs. No key needed for community access.

malwarebazaar.label: MalwareBazaar (abuse.ch)
malwarebazaar.desc: Hash lookup against known malware samples.

hibp.label: Have I Been Pwned
hibp.desc: Email address breach detection (requires HIBP API key).

security.title: Security
security.desc: Platform authentication policy. Applies to all accounts.
security.apply_hint: Policy applied to authentication: account lockout and password length immediately; session duration on next login; inactivity logout on next page load.

passwordMinLength.label: Minimum password length
passwordMinLength.desc: Minimum number of characters required when creating or changing an account.

lockoutThreshold.label: Lockout after N failures
lockoutThreshold.desc: Failed login attempts before account lockout (0 = disabled).

lockoutWindowMin.label: Lockout duration
lockoutWindowMin.desc: How long the account remains locked.

sessionDurationH.label: Session duration
sessionDurationH.desc: Session lifetime (refresh token) before a new login is required.

inactivityTimeoutMin.label: Inactivity logout
inactivityTimeoutMin.desc: Automatic logout after N minutes without activity (0 = disabled).

retention.title: Data retention
retention.desc: GDPR compliance — right to erasure (Art. 17). Permanent purge (DoD 5220.22-M overwrite, immutable audit retained under Art. 17(3)).
retention.enable_auto_desc: Disabled by default. When enabled, a daily cron purges eligible cases.
retention.delay_after_close_desc: A closed case older than N days becomes eligible. Cases under legal hold or exemption are always excluded.
retention.no_eligible: ✓ No cases are eligible for purge with this delay.
retention.preview_count: ⚠ {{count}} case(s) would be purged (closed for > {{days}} days):
retention.manual_desc: Purge a case immediately, or exempt it from automatic purge.

log.no_artifacts_detected: No artifacts detected in the archive
log.parsing_done: ✓ Parsing complete: {{count}} records — {{ok}} OK / {{skipped}} skipped / {{errors}} error(s)
log.creating_zip_from_files: Creating ZIP from files… (do not close this page)

errors.socket_required: Socket connection required — reload the page

docs.title: Heimdall Documentation
docs.subtitle: Everything needed to run an investigation: Windows & Linux artifacts, Event IDs, memory analysis, MITRE attack patterns, network forensics, tools, and DFIR methodology. Use
docs.english_fallback_body: This long-form section is being edited in English. The shell, navigation, and summaries are available in English; switch to French to read the complete current corpus.

attacks.label: Attack Patterns
attacks.desc: 35 patterns — MITRE ATT&CK, APT, Sigma rules

network-forensics.label: Network Forensics
network-forensics.desc: PCAP, beaconing, DNS, exfiltration, proxy logs

threat_hunt.title: Threat Hunting
threat_hunt.subtitle: Proactive detection with YARA rules (binaries) and Sigma rules (timeline).
threat_hunt.no_yara: No YARA rules. Create one to get started.
threat_hunt.no_sigma: No Sigma rules. Create one to get started.

github.title: Import from GitHub — {{type}}
github.select_repo_desc: Select a repository. All valid rules will be downloaded and imported in one batch.
github.confirm_desc_before: The repository ZIP will be downloaded directly from github.com (without API), extracted server-side, then each rule will be syntax-validated before insertion. The operation can take
github.importing_steps: ZIP download · Extraction · Validation · Database insertion

sysmon.intro_before: Reference community Sysmon configurations. Import fetches the XML server-side and stores it in the platform library for endpoint deployment (
sysmon.intro_after: ). A Sysmon configuration controls endpoint collection; log hunting is performed with Sigma rules.

swiftonsecurity.desc: Reference baseline, heavily commented and conservative. Recommended starting point for most Windows environments.

sysmon-modular.desc: Modular configuration mapped to MITRE ATT&CK. Broader detection coverage, more verbose.

neo23x0.desc: Fork of the SwiftOnSecurity baseline maintained by Florian Roth, with practical detection tweaks and fixes for threat hunting.

ion-storm.desc: Extended fork of the SwiftOnSecurity baseline with additional network rules and coverage.

sysmon-modular-filedelete.desc: sysmon-modular variant that archives deleted files (Event IDs 23/26), valuable against ransomware and anti-forensics but storage intensive.

run_all.intro_between: (YARA, Sigma, Hayabusa, and behavioral detections) on a case,
run_all.intro_after: — you can leave the page, the server-side job keeps running. Results are aggregated below.

yara.rules_count: {{count}} rule(s) — used to scan evidence files
yara.rules_count_plural: {{count}} rules — used to scan evidence files
yara.no_scan_results: No results — run a scan to analyze files

sigma.rules_count: {{count}} rule(s) — used for hunting in the Super Timeline
sigma.rules_count_plural: {{count}} rules — used for hunting in the Super Timeline

commandPalette.title: Command bar
commandPalette.placeholder: Navigate, search an IP / hash / case…

feedback.empty_my_requests: You have not submitted any requests yet.

globalAi.system_prompt:
You are a DFIR (Digital Forensics & Incident Response) expert and senior cybersecurity analyst.
You help the analyst understand forensic artifacts, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, and digital evidence.
Answer in English. Be precise, concise, and actionable.
If you do not know, say so clearly instead of inventing an answer.
globalAi.title: Forensic AI
globalAi.subtitle: Forensic copilot — ask a question or choose a topic
globalAi.empty_hint: Ask a question or select a prompt from the menu
globalAi.footer_hint: Shift+Enter = line break · data stays local
globalAi.edit_prompt_title: Put in the text box to edit

artefacts.label: Windows artifacts
prompts: Explain what the MFT ($MFT) contains and how to use it in forensics.
What are Prefetch files and what can we infer from them?
What information do LNK (.lnk) files contain?
How do you analyze Windows registry hives (SAM, SYSTEM, SOFTWARE)?
What are Shellbags and how can they be used to retrace navigation?
What does the EVTX event log contain and which Event IDs are critical?
Explain what AMCACHE is and how to detect suspicious executables.
How can SRUM artifacts be used to reconstruct network activity?

mitre.label: MITRE ATT&CK
prompts: Explain the most common persistence techniques (T1547) on Windows.
How do you detect process hollowing (T1055.012) in logs?
Which artifacts indicate lateral movement (T1021) via RDP or SMB?
Explain Pass-the-Hash (T1550.002) and the artifacts it leaves behind.
How can you identify data exfiltration (T1041) in network logs?
What is DLL sideloading (T1574.002) and how do you detect it?
Explain the most common privilege escalation techniques (T1068).
How do you detect Living Off the Land (LOLBins) in Windows logs?

detection.label: Detection & Analysis
prompts: Which Windows Event IDs are the most important for detecting a compromise?
How do you analyze a memory dump with Volatility to find malware?
Explain how to identify ransomware from forensic artifacts.
Which indicators show that an account was compromised in AD logs?
How do you detect a mimikatz/LSASS dump (Event IDs 4656, 4663)?
Explain how to reconstruct an incident timeline from timestamps.
Which YARA rules help detect common malware?
How do you detect a C2 beacon in a network timeline?

report.label: Report & Summary
prompts: Generate a forensic incident report template.
What should an executive cyber incident summary include?
How do you document the chain of custody for evidence?
Explain the STIX/TAXII format for sharing IOCs.
What should be included in a triage note for a NOC/SOC analyst?
How do you write an attack timeline for a non-technical CISO?

triage.title: Triage cockpit
triage.subtitle: What needs your attention now, across all cases, prioritized.

welcome.title: Welcome to Heimdall
welcome.desc: A short guided tour. On each page, I’ll show the essentials in a few seconds. You can skip at any time.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Overview: active cases, detected IOCs, SLA deadlines, and shortcuts. Your starting point every morning.

cases.title: Cases
cases.desc: Create and manage your forensic cases. Each case groups evidence, timelines, detections, IOCs, and reports.

collection.title: Collection
collection.desc: Generate a collection agent (KAPE, Velociraptor, CyLR…) then import the archive — artifacts are parsed automatically.

iocs.title: IOCs
iocs.desc: Auto-extracted and enriched indicators of compromise. Search a hash, IP, or domain across all cases.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.desc: Methodologies, playbooks, and references at hand during the investigation.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.desc: Deadlines and milestones for your cases. Urgent SLAs surface here and on the dashboard.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Preferences, SLA thresholds, detection rules, integrations, and YARA / Sigma rule management.

admin.title: Operations
admin.desc: System health, jobs, backups, Docker, AI model, and audit log. Create accounts without email, manage roles.

done.title: Ready to investigate
done.desc: You know the essentials. Open a case, import a collection, and let Heimdall correlate. Good hunting.

networkMap.no_lateral: No lateral movement detected for this node.

actions.false_positive_title: False positive — suppresses "{{value}}" (click = this case · Shift+click = global)

confidence.high_title: High confidence — very specific pattern, low false-positive rate
confidence.medium_title: Medium confidence — useful signal but should be corroborated
confidence.low_title: Low confidence — often legitimate, validate manually (e.g. recon, RDP)

header.intro: Automatic detection of adversary techniques in collected artifacts (MFT, EVTX, Sysmon, Hayabusa).
header.run_background_title: Runs YARA, Sigma, Hayabusa, and detections in the background (server-side — survives leaving the page)

timestomping.title: Timestomping ($SIA vs $FN)
timestomping.intro: Click Analyze to detect MFT timestamp inconsistencies ($SIA ≠ $FN).

double_ext.title: Double Extension (*.pdf.exe, *.docx.scr…)
double_ext.intro: Click Scan to look for files with dangerous double extensions.
double_ext.empty_subtitle: No file with a dangerous double extension was found.

beaconing.title: C2 beaconing (periodic connections)
beaconing.intro: Click Detect to analyze suspicious periodic connections (C2 beaconing).
beaconing.empty_subtitle: No suspicious periodic connection with this threshold.

persistence.title: Windows persistence (Registry, LNK, BITS, Hayabusa)
persistence.intro: Click Analyze to detect persistence mechanisms (Registry RunKeys, LNK Startup, BITS Jobs, Sigma Hayabusa rules).
persistence.empty_subtitle: No persistence mechanism found in the collected artifacts.

sysmon.title: Sysmon behavior (EventID 1/3/7/8/10/11/25)
sysmon.intro: Analyzes Sysmon logs to detect execution from %TEMP%, process injection (EID 8), LSASS access (EID 10), unsigned DLLs (EID 7), suspicious network connections (EID 3), and process tampering (EID 25).
sysmon.empty_subtitle: No Sysmon event matches known adversary techniques.

attack_tech.title: ATT&CK techniques (Credential Access · Lateral · Discovery)
attack_tech.intro: LSASS / Mimikatz dump, DCSync, Kerberoasting (RC4), remote execution (PsExec/WMI/WinRM), RDP and explicit credentials, and discovery (whoami /all, nltest, net group).

vuln_drivers.title: Vulnerable drivers (LOLDrivers) · DLL hijacking (HijackLibs)
vuln_drivers.intro: Compares Amcache drivers (SHA1) against the LOLDrivers repository (vulnerable/malicious drivers) and loaded DLLs outside their expected location (HijackLibs). Repositories are fetched server-side and cached for 24h — the first analysis can take a few seconds.

execution_anomaly.title: Execution anomalies (LOLBins · Masquerading · PowerShell)
execution_anomaly.intro: System binary abuse (regsvr32/mshta/certutil/bitsadmin…), masquerading (system process outside System32), and PowerShell abuse (encoded command, download cradle, hidden window).

anti_forensic.title: Anti-forensics (trace deletion)
anti_forensic.intro: Log clearing (EID 1102/104, wevtutil cl), Shadow Copy deletion (vssadmin/wmic), USN journal deletion, secure wiping (cipher/sdelete), Sysmon/EDR tampering, and Defender disabling.
